fields.name: code
fields.description:
Short code used to identify the activity within its context.

.Examples:
- _"TUT01"_
- _"LEC02"_

**Max length:** _31_ characters.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
Human-readable name of the activity.

.Examples:
- _"Tutorial 1"_
- _"Lecture Stream B"_

**Max length:** 127 characters.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
Longer description of the activity for display or admin use.

.Example:
- _"Advanced Algorithms"_

**Max length:** _255_ characters.

fields.name: activitySize
fields.description:
Configured size (number of seats/spaces) for this activity.

.Example:
- _30_

fields.name: planningCount
fields.description:
Planned (target) size used during scheduling or planning.

.Example:
- _32_

fields.name: realCount
fields.description:
The real size of the activity.   Actual (real/observed) count, e.g., measured or confirmed capacity.

.Example:
- _28_

fields.name: fullCode
fields.description:
The full Schedule Plus code of the activity.   Full Schedule Plus code (external system identifier) for cross-system mapping.

.Example:
- _"COMP1010_TUT01_2025_S1"_

fields.name: isScheduled
fields.description:
Whether this activity has been scheduled (i.e., has time/location assignments).

.States:
- _true_  = scheduled
- _false_ = not scheduled

fields.name: startTime
fields.description:
_(Deprecated)_ Start time as a local time value.

Replaced by _weekPatternDates_, which includes start and end times per date.

fields.name: endTime
fields.description:
_(Deprecated)_ End time as a local time value.

Replaced by _weekPatternDates_, which includes start and end times per date.

fields.name: dayOfWeek
fields.description:
_(Deprecated)_ Day of the week as a short string _(Mon, Tue, …)_.

Replaced by _weekPatternDates_, which provides concrete dates.

fields.name: publishedAllocate
fields.description:
Publication flag indicating if this activity should be sent to Allocate Plus.

.States:
- _true_  = published/sent
- _false_ = not published

fields.name: publishedInternal
fields.description:
Publication flag indicating if this activity should be sent to the Internal Publisher (institutional/internal timetables).

.States:
- _true_  = published/sent
- _false_ = not published

fields.name: typeCode
fields.description:
Code of the Activity Type.

.Examples:
- _"TUT"_
- _"LEC"_
- _"LAB"_

fields.name: typeName
fields.description:
Display name of the Activity Type.

.Examples:
- _"Tutorial"_
- _"Lecture"_
- _"Laboratory"_

fields.name: weekPatternDates
fields.description:
Event or actual occurrences of this activity (per teaching date), each with start and end timestamps.

Use this instead of the deprecated _startTime/endTime/dayOfWeek_.

.Example element:
- _eventStartDateTime_: _"2025-03-04 09:00"_
- _eventEndDateTime_:   _"2025-03-04 11:00"_

fields.name: assignedStaff
fields.description: A list of the staff assigned to this activity.

fields.name: assignedRoom
fields.description: A list of the rooms assigned to this activity (e.g., AV kits, lab gear).

fields.name: assignedLocation
fields.description: A list of the locations assigned to this activity.

fields.name: assignedEquipment
fields.description: A list of the equipment assigned to this activity.

fields.name: availableForAllocation
fields.description:
Allocate Plus: 'Available for Allocation' flag status.

Requires Activity to be published to Allocate Plus.

add.version: 25.4.0.2

NOTE: Co-teaching Activities result sets are provided as a logical OR - i.e., If any are true then returns true.

.Possible field returns:
- _True/False_ - As set in Allocate Plus
- _NULL_ - Activity is not published or cannot be found in Allocate Plus

fields.name: notes1
fields.description:
Free-form notes captured in the first activity notes field.
Typically used for additional context or internal remarks.

add.version: 25.6.1.0

fields.name: notes2
fields.description:
Free-form notes captured in the second activity notes field.
Can be used as an alternative or supplementary text field.

add.version: 25.6.1.0

fields.name: notes
fields.description:
Detailed or rolled up activity notes, stored as structured `Note` entries.
This is a nested type, that includes the data in the `notes1` and `notes2` fields.

add.version: 25.6.1.0

fields.name: assignedAttributes
fields.description:
List of attributes assigned to the activity.

add.version: 25.6.1.0

.Example:
- _"Recording Option"_
- _"Activity Category"_

fields.description:

- The `old` array contains the date before the change.
- The `new` array contains the date after the change.
- Example: `"YYYY-MM-DD format."`

enumValues.name: ADD
enumValues.description:
Indicates that a new item (entity, object or logical object) was created (`added`) to the system.

• In a delta query context, this value signals that the returned record did not previously exist
and should be treated as a brand‐new object.
• In a search filter context, specifying `ADD` will limit the results to only those items that have been newly created
(and not to those that were merely updated or deleted) during the search window.

Examples:
(between: DateFilter, changeType: ChangeType,
.To fetch only newly created records since your last sync:
[source,kotlin]
----
query {
  deltaChanges(operation: ADD) {
     old
		new
  }
}
----

enumValues.name: UPDATE
enumValues.description:
Indicates that an existing item was modified ("updated").

• In a delta query, this means that one or more fields of an existing entity have changed.
• In a search filter, using `UPDATE` restricts results to only those entities whose data has changed,
excluding brand‐new items (`ADD`) and items that were deleted (`DELETE`).

Additional notes:
• If multiple fields were updated in one transaction, the server will only emit a single `UPDATE` operation
for that entity; it does not break out one operation per field.
• If a client wishes to know exactly which fields changed, a local (to customer) comparison can be made against the `old` value with the `new` value
via a local JSON diff‐style payload (if supported by customer).

enumValues.name: DELETE
enumValues.description:
Indicates that an existing item was removed ("deleted") from the system.

• In a delta query, this tells clients that the record in question no longer exists in the primary data store.
• In a search filter, choosing `DELETE` will return only those entities that have been deleted or marked as deleted via the “soft‐delete”, during the search interval.

NOTE Customer should be aware that the PLUS Suite makes use of “soft deletes” in most cases, compare the `validTo` field to identify when the item is "soft deleted".

inputFields.name: fromDateTime
inputFields.description:
.**Required:** Inclusive start or lower bound of the date/time window.
* **Format:** ISO-8601 `YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:mm:ss` (e.g. _2025-01-01T00:00:00_).
* **Constraint:** Must be less than or equal to `toDateTime`.
* Any event whose timestamp is exactly equal to `after` must be included in the result.

.Validation:
* If `after` is missing or not a valid ISO-8601 string, return a validation error: +
`"Invalid ISO8601DateTime format for field 'after'."`

.Examples:
* `2025-05-01T00:00:00` (midnight UTC on May 1, 2025)
* `2025-06-06T14:30:15`
* `2025-06-06T00:00:00Z`
* `2025-06-06T10:00:00+10:00`


If this field is not strictly earlier than or equal to `toDateTime`, the server will reject the request.

inputFields.name: toDateTime
inputFields.description:
.An explicit inclusive upper bound orr end of the date/time window.
* **Format:** ISO-8601 YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:mm:ss (e.g. 2025-05-31T23:59:59).
* **Constraint:** Must be greater than or equal to `fromDateTime`.

Example:
• `2025-05-31T23:59:59` (one second before midnight UTC on May 31, 2025)

If this field is not strictly later than or equal to fromDateTime, the server will reject the request.

inputFields.name: after
inputFields.description:
Inclusive lower bound of the date‐time window.

* Format: `ISO8601DateTime` (e.g. `2025-07-01T00:00:00Z`, `2025-07-01T10:00:00+02:00`).
* Constraint: Must be less than or equal to before.
* Behavior: Any event with a timestamp exactly equal to after will be included.

If this field is missing or cannot be parsed, return a validation error.
If this value is later than before, return:
"Field 'after' must be less than or equal to field 'before'."

inputFields.name: before
inputFields.description:
.Optional
*	an explicit inclusive upper bound of the date‐time window.
* Format: `ISO8601DateTime` (e.g. `2025-07-31T23:59:59Z`, `2025-07-31T23:59:59+10:00`).
* Any event whose timestamp is exactly equal to `before` must be included in the result.

.Constraints:
1. If `before` is provided, **`tillMidnight` must be omitted or false**. Otherwise, return: +
`"Fields 'before' and 'tillMidnight' cannot both be provided."`
2. After both `after` and `before` are known, the server enforces `after ≤ before`. If not, return: +
`"Field 'before' must be greater than or equal to field 'after'."`


.Example:
* `"2025-06-06T23:59:59-05:00"` (end of day in UTC–5).

.Constraint: Must be greater than or equal to after.
* Behavior: Any event with a timestamp exactly equal to before will be included.

If this field is missing or cannot be parsed, return a validation error.
If this value is earlier than after, return:
"Field 'before' must be greater than or equal to field 'after'."

inputFields.name: tillMidnight
inputFields.description:
.Optional
* If set to `true`, the server will treat this as a request to fetch up to the end of the “calendar day” of `after`.

.The server calculates:
* `computedBefore = (timestamp of 'after' truncated to date) + 23:59:59.999…`	in the same offset as `after`.
* In other words, if `after = "2025-06-06T10:15:00+10:00"`, then `computedBefore = "2025-06-06T23:59:59.999+10:00"`. (end of day)

.Constraints:
1. If `tillMidnight` is true, **`before` must be null**. Otherwise an error will be returned: +
`"Fields 'before' and 'tillMidnight' must not both be provided."`
2. If `tillMidnight` is omitted or false, then `before` **must** be provided. Otherwise, return: +
`"Either 'before' or 'tillMidnight' must be provided alongside 'after'."`
3. After computing `computedBefore`, enforce `after ≤ computedBefore`. (This is always true by construction.)

.Default:
* The default value is `false`. If neither `before` nor `tillMidnight` is provided, the server will return a validation error because one of these is required.

fields.name: changeTime
fields.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp (`YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:mm:ss`) when the change was recorded.
* Represented as a `LocalDateTime`.
* All times are stored and returned in UTC.
* Clients can use this to order or paginate change events.

fields.name: changeType
fields.description:
The category of entity that was changed (e.g., `DEPARTMENT`, `SUBJECT`, `ACTIVITY_GROUP`, `ACTIVITY`).
* See the `ChangeType` enum for all possible values.
* Use this to filter or route change events based on entity type.

fields.name: changeOperation
fields.description:
The operation performed on the entity.

.Operations:
* `ADD`    → A new entity was created; `oldValue` will be `null`.
* `UPDATE` → An existing entity’s fields were modified; both `oldValue` and `newValue` will be non-null.
* `DELETE` → An entity was removed; `newValue` will be `null`.
* See the `ChangeOperation` enum for details.

fields.name: oldValue
fields.description:
`JSON` representation of the entity’s state **before** the change.

* For `ADD` operations, this field is always `null`.
* For `UPDATE` and `DELETE` operations, this contains the full entity as it existed prior to modification or removal.
* The JSON structure corresponds to the entity’s schema (e.g., all department attributes or subject fields).

fields.name: newValue
fields.description:
`JSON` representation of the entity’s state **after** the change.

* For `DELETE` operations, this field is always `null`.
* For `ADD` and `UPDATE` operations, this contains the full entity as it exists after the change.
* Consumers can merge `oldValue` and `newValue` to compute diffs if needed.

fields.name: uploadFile
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: Upload File to S3**

Uploads a file to a specified S3 bucket. This mutation accepts the S3 bucket name, the key (i.e. file name/path) under which the file should be stored, and the file itself as an Upload scalar (multipart file). Upon success, it returns a confirmation message (typically including the file key).

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Parameters:
- **bucket**: The name of the S3 bucket where the file will be uploaded.
- **key**: The S3 key (i.e., the unique file identifier) under which the file will be stored.
- **file**: The file to be uploaded. Must conform to the GraphQL multipart request specification.

Returns:
- A String confirmation message indicating the file upload status.

fields.name: deleteFile
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: Delete File from S3**

Deletes a file from a specified S3 bucket using the provided key. This mutation is used for removing files stored in S3 and returns a confirmation message upon successful deletion.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Parameters:
- **bucket**: The name of the S3 bucket containing the file.
- **key**: The S3 key (i.e., file identifier) of the file to delete.

Returns:
- A String confirmation message indicating that the file has been successfully deleted.

fields.name: moveFile
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: Move File within S3**

Moves a file from one location to another within S3. This operation typically involves copying the file from the source location to the destination location and then deleting the original file. The mutation returns a confirmation message upon successful completion of the move operation.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Parameters:
- **sourceBucket**: The name of the S3 bucket containing the original file.
- **sourceKey**: The S3 key of the file to move.
- **destinationBucket**: The name of the target S3 bucket for the file.
- **destinationKey**: The S3 key for the file at its new location.

Returns:
- A String confirmation message indicating that the file has been successfully moved.

inputFields.name: publishedInternal
inputFields.description:
**Internal Publication Status (OR Logic Component)**
	
Controls filtering based on the internal publication flag as part of an OR condition.
Maps to `activity_part_base.published_int` database field.
	
**⚠️ OR Logic**: When combined with `publishedAllocate`, uses OR logic:
Results include activity parts that match THIS condition OR the `publishedAllocate` condition.
	
- `true`: Include activity parts that are internally published (published_int = 'Y') OR match `publishedAllocate`
- `false`: Include activity parts that are not published (published_int = 'N') OR match `publishedAllocate`
- `null`: Ignore this field in filtering (only `publishedAllocate` applies)
- **Default**: `true` (published activities OR allocated activities)
	
**Individual Field Behavior:**
- When `publishedAllocate` is `null`: Acts as standalone filter for this field only
- When both fields specified: Combined with OR logic
	
**Use Cases:**
- `true` - Include published activities (plus any that match allocation criteria)
- `false` - Include draft activities (plus any that match allocation criteria)
- `null` - Ignore publication status, rely only on allocation criteria

inputFields.name: publishedAllocate
inputFields.description:
**Allocation Registration Status (OR Logic Component)**
	
Controls filtering based on the class registration flag as part of an OR condition.
Maps to `activity_part_base.class_reg` database field.
	
**⚠️ OR Logic**: When combined with `publishedInternal`, uses OR logic:
Results include activity parts that match THIS condition OR the `publishedInternal` condition.
	
- `true`: Include activity parts that are registered (class_reg = 'Y') OR match `publishedInternal`
- `false`: Include activity parts that are not registered (class_reg = 'N') OR match `publishedInternal`
- `null`: Ignore this field in filtering (only `publishedInternal` applies)
- **Default**: `true` (registered activities OR published activities)
	
**Individual Field Behavior:**
- When `publishedInternal` is `null`: Acts as standalone filter for this field only
- When both fields specified: Combined with OR logic
	
**Use Cases:**
- `true` - Include allocated activities (plus any that match publication criteria)
- `false` - Include planning activities (plus any that match publication criteria)
- `null` - Ignore allocation status, rely only on publication criteria

fields.name: getSubjects

Fetch a paginated list of `Subject` objects, with optional filtering by subject
code(s), SMS attributes, timetable period, and department.

fields.name: internalListIngressFiles
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: List Files from S3 Ingress Folder.**

This query retrieves a list of S3 objects within a PLUS Suite ingress folder in the customer S3 bucket. Each returned object includes metadata such as the S3 key, file size, last modified timestamp, and ETag. This operation is intended for internal usage and is restricted to JDR-only access.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

fields.name: listFiles
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: List Files from S3**

This query retrieves a list of S3 objects within a specific folder in the designated S3 bucket. Each returned object includes metadata such as the S3 key, file size, last modified timestamp, and ETag. This operation is intended for internal usage and is restricted to JDR-only access.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Parameters:
- **bucket**: The name of the S3 bucket from which files will be listed.
- **folder**: The folder prefix used to filter the files. It is recommended to include a trailing slash (e.g., "folder/") to list only files within that folder.

Returns:
- A list of S3ObjectDetails, where each item represents the metadata of an S3 file.

fields.name: getFile
fields.description:
**INTERNAL: Get File from S3**

This query retrieves the full content and key of an S3 file. The file is identified by its bucket and key, and the returned result includes both the S3 key and the file content as a string. This operation is designed for internal usage and is restricted to JDR-only access.

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Parameters:
- **bucket**: The name of the S3 bucket that contains the file.
- **key**: The unique S3 key identifying the file.

Returns:
- An S3FileDetails object containing: **key**: The S3 key of the file and **content**: The complete content of the file, represented as a String.

fields.description:
**INTERNAL**:

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

add.version 25.2.0

Fetch a paginated list of change records for a particular entity category (`ChangeType`) that occurred within the specified date window (`between`).
Optionally filter those records by the type of operation (`ChangeOperation`: `ADD`, `UPDATE`, or `DELETE`).

- `between` : the list of full activity codes to fetch. _Required_
- `changeType` : change type. _Required_
- `operation` : the operation type. _Optional_
- `pageNumber` :  the page number to fetch, defaults to zero
- `pageSize` :  the number of items per page, defaults to 10
- _RETURNS_ :  a list of Activity objects corresponding to the provided codes


.Use cases:
* **Incremental synchronisation:** clients can pull only the changes for a given entity type since their last checkpoint.
* **Selective replays**: if a client only cares about deletions or updates for a specific entity, then pass the `operation` filter.

.Parameters:
* `between: DateFilter!`
** Defines the inclusive time window for which to return change records.
** `DateFilter` contains two fields: `startDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp) and `endDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp). +
+
.Example:
[source,kotlin]
----
DateFilter {
 startDateTime: LocalDateTime!    // e.g. "2025-05-01T00:00:00"
 endDateTime:   LocalDateTime!    // e.g. "2025-06-01T23:59:59"
}
----
+
* `changeType: ChangeType!`
**  Specifies the logical category of entity whose changes you want to retrieve (e.g. `DEPARTMENT`, `SUBJECT`, `ACTIVITY_GROUP`, `ACTIVITY`).
** This ensures the server only returns change events for that particular object group.

* `operation: ChangeOperation` (optional)
** When provided, further restricts the result set to changes of that kind:
*** `ADD`   → Newly created entities
*** `UPDATE` → Existing entities that were modified
*** `DELETE` → Entities that were removed
** If omitted, returns all operations (ADD, UPDATE, DELETE) for the specified `changeType`.

* `pageNumber: Int! = 0`
** Zero-based index of the page to fetch. Default is `0` (first page).
** Used in combination with `pageSize` to paginate large result sets.

* `pageSize: Int! = 10`
** Maximum number of `DeltaChange` records to return per page. Default is `10`.
** Adjust this upward if clients need to pull more records in a single call; keep it modest to avoid large payloads.

.Returns:
* `[DeltaChange]`
** A list of change records matching the filters. Each `DeltaChange` typically contains:
** TODO add the return fields.

.Example Queries:
1. Fetch all DEPARTMENT changes (any operation) between May 1 and May 31, 2025, first page of 20 items: +
[source,kotlin]
----
query {
 getDeltaChanges(
  between: {
   fromDateTime: "2025-05-01T00:00:00Z",
   toDateTime:   "2025-05-31T23:59:59Z"
  },
  changeType: DEPARTMENT,
  pageNumber: 0,
  pageSize: 20
  ) {
   oldValue
   newValue
  }
}
----

fields.name: getActivityDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData
fields.description:
**INTERNAL**:

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

add.version 25.2.0

Fetch a paginated list of activity delta change records from the optimized `gql_delta_data` table within a specified date window.
This query provides enhanced performance compared to `getActivityDeltaChanges` by using the dedicated delta data table instead of the message queue.

- `between`: The time window to filter the results by. _Required_
- `pageNumber`: The page number to fetch, defaults to 0
- `pageSize`: The number of items per page, defaults to 10
- _RETURNS_: A list of ActivityDeltaChange objects containing detailed delta information

.Parameters:
* `between: DateFilter!`
** Defines the inclusive time window for which to return activity change records.
** `DateFilter` contains two fields: `fromDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp) and `toDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp).

.Example:
[source,graphql]
----
query {
	getActivityDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData(
		between: {
			fromDateTime: "2025-01-01T00:00:00",
			toDateTime: "2025-01-01T23:59:59"
		},
		pageNumber: 0,
		pageSize: 50
	) {
		unitCode
		unitTeachingPeriod
		effectiveDate
		eventDates {
			oldValue
			newValue
			hasChanged
		}
		assignedStaff {
			oldValue { code name }
			newValue { code name }
			hasChanged
		}
	}
}
----

.Returns:
* `[ActivityDeltaChange]`
** A list of activity change records with detailed delta information including old/new values for:
*** Event dates and times
*** Staff and room assignments
*** Day of week changes
*** Record status modifications

fields.name: getSimDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData
fields.description:
**INTERNAL**:

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

add.version 25.2.0

Fetch a paginated list of SIM delta change records from the optimized `gql_delta_data` table within a specified date window,
with optional campus and publication status filtering. This query provides enhanced performance by using the dedicated
delta data table and JSON-based campus filtering instead of complex table joins.

- `between`: The time window to filter the results by. _Required_
- `campusCode`: The campus code to filter by, defaults to "SIM"
- `publishedInternal`: Filter by internal publication status, defaults to true
- `publishedAllocate`: Filter by allocation publication status, defaults to true
- `pageNumber`: The page number to fetch, defaults to 0
- `pageSize`: The number of items per page, defaults to 10
- _RETURNS_: A list of SIMDeltaChange objects

.Parameters:
* `between: DateFilter!`
** Defines the inclusive time window for which to return SIM change records.
** `DateFilter` contains two fields: `fromDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp) and `toDateTime` (ISO 8601 timestamp).

* `campusCode: String = "SIM"`
** Optional campus code filter. Uses JSON data field `subject_campus` for filtering.
** Defaults to "SIM" if not specified.

* `publishedInternal: Boolean = true`
** Filter by internal publication status from `activity_part_base.published_int`.
** Defaults to `true` (only published internally).

* `publishedAllocate: Boolean = true`
** Filter by allocation publication status from `activity_part_base.class_reg`.
** Defaults to `true` (only class registered).

.Example:
[source,graphql]
----
query {
	getSimDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData(
		between: {
			fromDateTime: "2025-01-01T00:00:00",
			toDateTime: "2025-01-01T23:59:59"
		},
		campusCode: "MAIN",
		publishedInternal: true,
		publishedAllocate: false,
		pageNumber: 0,
		pageSize: 50
	) {
		unitCode
		unitTeachingPeriod
		unitCampus
		effectiveDate
	}
}
----

fields.name: getSimDeltaChanges
fields.description:
Fetch a paginated list of change records that occurred within the specified date window (`between`) Default to today..

add.version 25.2.4

- `between`: The time window to filter the results by. _Required_
- `smsFilter`: SMS criteria filter for unit codes, teaching periods, classes, campuses, years, and modes. _Optional_
- `published`: Publication status filter for internal and allocation publication. _Optional_
- `pageNumber`: The page number to fetch, defaults to 0
- `pageSize`: The number of items per page, defaults to 100
- _RETURNS_: A list of SIMDeltaChange objects matching the filter criteria

[NOTE]
====
The following is applied to this query:
- If `SMSFilter` is not provided, `unitCampus` defaults to `SIM`.
- If `PublishedFilter` is not provided, both `publishedInternal` and `publishedAllocate` default to `true`.
====


.Parameters:
* `between: DateFilter!`
** Defines the inclusive time window for which to return SIM change records.

* `smsFilter: SMSFilter` (optional)
** Comprehensive SMS criteria filter with the following fields:
*** `unitCode: String` - Filter by unit codes (subject codes)
*** `unitTeachingPeriod: String` - Filter by teaching periods
*** `unitClass: String` - Filter by unit classes
*** `unitCampus: String` - Filter by campus codes
*** `unitYear: Int` - Filter by unit years
*** `subjectMode: String` - Filter by subject modes

* `published: PublishedFilter` (optional)
** Publication status filter with the following fields:
*** `publishedInternal: Boolean = true` - Internal publication status
*** `publishedAllocate: Boolean = true` - Allocation publication status

.Example: With defaults applied.
[source,graphql]
----
query {
getSimDeltaChanges(
		between: {
			fromDateTime: "2025-01-01T00:00:00",
			toDateTime: "2025-01-01T23:59:59"
		},
		smsFilter: {
      unitCampus: "SIM"
		},
		published: {
			publishedInternal: true,
			publishedAllocate: true
		},
		pageNumber: 0,
		pageSize: 100
	) {
		unitCode
		unitTeachingPeriod
		unitCampus
		unitYear
		effectiveDate
	}
}
----

fields.name: filterActivityDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData
fields.description:
**INTERNAL**:

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

add.version 25.2.0

Advanced filtering of activity delta changes using SMS criteria and publication filters stored in JSON data fields.
This is the most flexible and powerful activity delta query, providing the same filtering capabilities as the SIM version
but returning detailed `ActivityDeltaChange` data structures with comprehensive old/new value tracking.

- `between`: The time window to filter the results by. _Required_
- `smsFilter`: SMS criteria filter for unit codes, teaching periods, classes, campuses, years, and modes. _Optional_
- `published`: Publication status filter for internal and allocation publication. _Optional_
- `pageNumber`: The page number to fetch, defaults to 0
- `pageSize`: The number of items per page, defaults to 10
- _RETURNS_: A list of ActivityDeltaChange objects with detailed delta information

.Parameters:
* `between: DateFilter!`
** Defines the inclusive time window for which to return activity change records.

* `smsFilter: SMSFilter` (optional)
** Comprehensive SMS criteria filter with the following fields:
*** `unitCode: [String]` - Filter by unit codes (subject codes)
*** `unitTeachingPeriod: [String]` - Filter by teaching periods
*** `unitClass: [String]` - Filter by unit classes
*** `unitCampus: [String]` - Filter by campus codes
*** `unitYear: [Int]` - Filter by unit years
*** `subjectMode: [String]` - Filter by subject modes

* `published: PublishedFilter` (optional)
** Publication status filter with the following fields:
*** `publishedInternal: Boolean = true` - Internal publication status
*** `publishedAllocate: Boolean = true` - Allocation publication status

.Delta Information Available:
ActivityDeltaChange objects provide rich delta tracking including:
* `eventDates` - Changes to scheduled event dates with detailed change lists
* `startTime` / `endTime` - Changes to activity timing
* `dayOfWeek` - Changes to scheduled days
* `assignedRoom` - Room assignment changes with full resource details
* `assignedStaff` - Staff assignment changes with full resource details
* `recordStatus` - Activity status changes (ACTIVE, CANCELLED, etc.)

.Example:
[source,graphql]
----
query {
	filterActivityDeltaChangesViaGqlDeltaData(
		between: {
			fromDateTime: "2025-01-01T00:00:00",
			toDateTime: "2025-01-01T23:59:59"
		},
		smsFilter: {
			unitCode: ["CS101", "MATH201"],
			unitTeachingPeriod: ["S1", "S2"],
			unitCampus: ["MAIN"]
		},
		published: {
			publishedInternal: true,
			publishedAllocate: false
		},
		pageNumber: 0,
		pageSize: 100
	) {
		unitCode
		unitTeachingPeriod
		unitCampus
		effectiveDate
		eventDates {
			oldValue
			newValue
			hasChanged
			changes
		}
		assignedRoom {
			oldValue { code name displayName }
			newValue { code name displayName }
			hasChanged
		}
		assignedStaff {
			oldValue { code name displayName }
			newValue { code name displayName }
			hasChanged
		}
		recordStatus {
			oldValue
			newValue
			hasChanged
		}
	}
}
----

fields.name: getIntegrationStatus
fields.description:
**INTERNAL**:

**PERMISSION**: JDR-ONLY

Retrieves integration status for the specified external reference IDs and marks pending messages as processed.
This query provides status information for integration processes and automatically dequeues any pending messages.

add.version: 25.2.4.1

- `extRefIds`: List of external reference IDs to query status for. _Required_
- _RETURNS_: A list of IntegrationOutcome objects representing the status of each integration

.Parameters:
* `extRefIds: [String!]!`
** List of external reference identifiers to retrieve status for.
** These correspond to the extRefId values used when creating integration messages.
** Must not be null or empty.

.Returns:
* `[IntegrationOutcome]`
** A list of integration outcomes with the following information:
*** `extRefId` - External reference identifier
*** `integrationProcessId` - Unique process identifier
*** `status` - Current status (PENDING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED)
*** `info` - Count of informational messages
*** `warnings` - Count of warning messages
*** `errors` - Count of error messages
*** `startTime` - When the integration process started
*** `endTime` - When the integration process completed

.Side Effects:
This query automatically marks any pending integration messages as processed by:
* Setting `actTime` to the current timestamp
* Setting `actStatus` to '0' (processed)

.Example:
[source,graphql]
----
query {
	getIntegrationStatus(extRefIds: ["batch-123", "batch-456"]) {
		extRefId
		integrationProcessId
		status
		info
		warnings
		errors
		startTime
		endTime
	}
}
----

fields.name: getActivityTypes
fields.description:
Fetches a list of activity types with pagination support.

Activity types represent the classification of teaching activities in the timetabling system,
such as Lectures (LEC), Tutorials (TUT), Labs (LAB), Clinics (CLI), and other educational
activity formats. Each activity type defines characteristics including availability across
departments and timetable periods.

.Use this query when you need to:
- Display a list of available activity types for selection in UI components
- Retrieve activity type metadata including codes, names, and descriptions
- Page through activity types for administrative purposes
- Understand which activity types are available across departments or timetable periods

add.version: 25.7.0.0

.Arguments:
- `pageNumber`: the page number to fetch, defaults to zero
- `pageSize`: the number of items per page, defaults to 10
- _RETURNS_: a list of ActivityType objects ordered by code

.Example Query:
[source,graphql]
----
query {
	getActivityTypes(pageNumber: 0, pageSize: 10) {
		code
		name
		description
		allDepartments
		allTimetablePeriods
		note
		display
	}
}
----

fields.name: operationBatchId
fields.description:
Correlation ID shared by **all rows** in the same mutation request.

Usually a Version 7 temporal UUID (UUIDv7) that uniquely identifies the batch.
Example: "018ec2fc-3d42-7483-aadf-4eab71a507d3"

Use this value to trace or audit all rows that belong to one mutation call.

fields.name: operationRowId
fields.description:
Correlation ID for **this specific row** within the batch.

Usually a Version 7 temporal UUID (UUIDv7) that uniquely identifies the row.

This helps match the response back to the record that was submitted.

.Example:
- "018ec2fc-3d42-7484-ae91-efb4c4236ba2"
- "ROW_7_ACT101_1122334455"

fields.name: inputPayload
fields.description:
Echo of the submitted payload (or subset) for this row.

Useful for audit logging or debugging. Typically represented as JSON.

.Example:
- _"{ \"code\": \"TUT01\", \"capacity\": 30 }"_

fields.name: errors
fields.description:
General errors related to this operation row.

- For `SYNC` operations, includes both validation and runtime errors.
- For `ASYNC` operations, includes only immediate/pre-flight issues.

Example: ["Duplicate key: Activity(code=TUT01) already exists"]

fields.name: mode
fields.description:
The mode in which this operation will be performed.
Either `SYNC` for synchronous or `ASYNC` for asynchronous.

fields.name: operation
fields.description:
The requested operation type for this data mutation.

Possible values: `CREATE`, `UPDATE`, `REJECT`, or `ACCEPT`.

fields.name: changes
fields.description: The changes that were accepted.

fields.name: validationErrors
fields.description: The changes failed validation.
